pattern: Two running totals -- one over the numbers themselves, one over every digit that appears in them -- answer the question in a single linear pass, no sorting or extra storage required.
hints: Keep two separate running totals as you scan the array once: the plain sum of the numbers, and a second sum that only cares about individual digits.
To add a number's digits to the digit total, repeatedly peel off the last digit (value modulo 10) and shrink the number (integer divide by 10) until nothing is left.
Once both totals are collected, the answer is just the absolute value of their difference -- watch out for which total ends up bigger, it isn't always the element sum.

edge_inputs.description: single element at the minimum value
single element at the maximum value
mix of one and two digit numbers
all single-digit numbers, so element sum equals digit sum and the difference is zero
round numbers with trailing zeros, where the digit sum collapses far below the element sum
a large value next to a small one to widen the gap between the two totals

stress.description: near-maximum-length array of random values across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 2000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
